stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In the 1940s, the Brazilian National Coffee Institute sought to reduce the country’s losses from a destructive coffee-leaf disease.
  • The institute enlisted plant breeder Helena Costa to develop a disease-resistant coffee plant.
  • She crossbred the susceptible commercial species Coffea arabica with wild coffee relatives from Africa.
  • She succeeded in producing coffee hybrids that resisted the disease while preserving the desired flavor profile.
stem: The student wants to emphasize Helena Costa’s achievement. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Brazilian National Coffee Institute, in the 1940s, sought to reduce the country’s losses from a destructive coffee-leaf disease.
Helena Costa succeeded in producing coffee hybrids that resisted a destructive leaf disease while preserving the desired flavor profile.
Helena Costa, a plant breeder, crossbred the species Coffea arabica with wild coffee relatives from Africa.
Helena Costa was enlisted by the Brazilian National Coffee Institute to develop a disease-resistant coffee plant.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes Costa’s achievement by foregrounding her success in producing disease-resistant coffee hybrids that preserved the desired flavor.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes the institute’s aim, not Costa’s achievement. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes Costa’s method but not the achievement that resulted. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes Costa’s task but not the achievement that resulted.
